sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Uptick for Life Sciences in Region Continues with Malvern-Based Ocugen's $22.5 Million Capital Raise
feed.summary: Malvern-based Ocugen recently secured $22.5 million in a stock offering, contributing to over $250 million raised by Philadelphia-area life sciences companies this year. The funding, led by RTW Investments, will support general corporate purposes, capital expenditures, and Ocugen's subsidiary OrthoCellix and its NeoCart product candidate. This capital raise signals an end to the "biotech winter," according to local entrepreneur Bruce Peacock, indicating a recovery period for the industry.

feed.title: Target, UnitedHealth, 3M, General Mills CEOs finally spoke up about ICE after weeks of silence. Here's what they said
feed.summary: After weeks of silence, over 60 Minnesota-based CEOs, including those from Target, UnitedHealth, 3M, and General Mills, released a collective letter on January 25th expressing concern about ICE activities in the Twin Cities. This statement followed the fatal shooting of Minneapolis resident Alex Pretti by a U.S. Border Patrol officer and came weeks after another ICE-related death. Many Minnesotans view this corporate response as delayed, contrasting sharply with the proactive stance taken by these same companies during George Floyd's murder in 2020.

feed.title: Research Alert: CFRA Lowers Opinion On Shares Of Walmart Inc. To Buy From Strong Buy
feed.summary: CFRA has downgraded its opinion on Walmart Inc. shares from "Strong Buy" to "Buy." This research alert from MT Newswires indicates a moderated outlook for the retail giant. Separately, Unity Software reported its Q4 financial results, with revenue increasing 35% year-over-year to $609 million, exceeding estimates, despite reporting a quarterly loss of 66 cents per share.

feed.title: Coherent’s Upcoming Earnings: A Key Test for AI-Driven Growth
feed.summary: Coherent Corp. is set to release its Q2 fiscal year 2026 earnings on February 4, 2026, after market close, covering the period ending December 31, 2025. Investors are focused on its performance within the AI infrastructure sector, given strong demand for its optical components used in AI data centers and communication networks. The report will confirm if the company has maintained its positive trajectory and growth driven by AI-related operations, building on previous optimistic forecasts for fiscal year 2026.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Appoints AWS Exec Faraz Shafiq as Head of AI Products and Solutions
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has appointed Faraz Shafiq, an executive from Amazon Web Services (AWS), as its new head of AI products and solutions, effective February 9. Shafiq will be responsible for the vision, roadmap, and development of AI-powered products across the bank, reporting to Saul Van Beurden, head of AI and co-CEO of Consumer Banking and Lending. This strategic hire underscores Wells Fargo's commitment to leveraging AI to transform its operations and enhance customer service, building on previous initiatives like training employees and expanding collaboration with Google Cloud.

feed.title: IDEX Corporation: Value Creator Is Not Delivering (NYSE:IEX)
feed.summary: IDEX Corporation, despite being a mission-critical industrial solutions provider with a long track record, is experiencing stagnant organic growth and lackluster performance. The company's earnings have remained flat, with cautious outlooks for upcoming years, leading to a compression in its valuation. The author remains cautious and believes sustained improvement in organic growth and earnings is necessary to justify even the reduced valuation.

feed.title: Horizon Kinetics buys Texas Pacific Land (TPL) share at $350
feed.summary: Horizon Kinetics Asset Management LLC, a significant shareholder in Texas Pacific Land Corp (NYSE:TPL), has purchased 1 share of the company's common stock for $350.06. This transaction occurred on January 23, 2026, slightly above the current trading price. The article also touches upon TPL's recent Q3 2025 earnings miss, a new partnership with Bolt Data & Energy, and a forthcoming three-for-one stock split.

feed.title: Northern Trust Leaders to Participate in BofA Securities 2026 Financial Services Conference on February 10
feed.summary: Northern Trust Corporation announced that CFO Dave Fox and Northern Trust Asset Management President Michael Hunstad will participate in the BofA Securities 2026 Financial Services Conference on February 10, 2026. A live webcast and replay of their presentation will be available on Northern Trust's investor relations website. The company also provided an overview of its global financial services, assets under custody/administration, and assets under management.

feed.title: A Look At Southwest Airlines (LUV) Valuation As Model Overhaul And Earnings Update Draw Investor Focus
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines (LUV) is undergoing a significant model overhaul, introducing assigned seating, premium cabins, and redeye flights, amidst challenges like rising labor costs and delivery delays. While analysts suggest the stock is slightly overvalued at US$41.95 based on earnings forecasts, a discounted cash flow model suggests a much higher intrinsic value of US$150.53, indicating a potential mispricing. Investors are weighing the impact of these changes on future earnings and growth, with an upcoming earnings webcast expected to provide further clarity.

feed.title: Analysts eye Caesars Entertainment stock closely amid mixed outlook and volatility
feed.summary: Analysts are closely watching Caesars Entertainment (NASDAQ: CZR) stock due to conflicting ratings, earnings revisions, significant debt, and share price volatility. While some see potential undervaluation, others are concerned about the heavy debt load, modest free cash flow, and downward earnings revisions. The firm's performance will depend on its ability to stabilize operations, improve margins, and capitalize on digital wagering growth amidst broader industry expansion.

feed.title: MGM Resorts Reaches 100% Daytime Solar Power on the Las Vegas Strip
feed.summary: MGM Resorts International has achieved 100% daytime solar power for its Las Vegas Strip properties, thanks to the new Escape Solar and Storage Project. This initiative, combined with their existing Mega Solar Array, significantly expands their renewable energy capacity and furthers their goal of 100% domestic renewable electricity by 2030. The move not only reduces environmental footprint but also aims to lower long-term energy costs and mitigate price volatility.

feed.title: Generac (GNRC) Stock Slips As Storm Rally Fades
feed.summary: Generac (GNRC) stock is trading lower after a winter storm that caused widespread power outages, despite such events typically boosting demand for power-backup solutions. Traders are likely locking in profits after the initial rally. Generac, a leader in U.S. home standby generators, sees these outages as real-time advertisements, expanding its addressable market and driving recurring revenue as it also expands into battery storage and microgrids.

feed.title: Principal Financial Group CEO Strable-Soethout sells $742k in shares
feed.summary: Deanna D. Strable-Soethout, CEO of Principal Financial Group Inc, sold 8,025 shares for approximately $742,794 on January 22, 2026, under a pre-arranged trading plan. This transaction leaves her with 54,227 indirectly owned shares. The sale follows a positive third-quarter earnings report for 2025, where the company exceeded analyst expectations, though JPMorgan recently downgraded the stock citing better value elsewhere in the life insurance sector.

feed.title: Watershed Country Music Festival Canceled For 2026
feed.summary: The Watershed Country Music Festival, held annually at the Gorge Amphitheater in Washington State, has been canceled for 2026 after 13 years. Organizer Live Nation Entertainment announced a hiatus on January 23rd, without specifying a reason for the cancellation, despite the festival drawing significant crowds. Future plans for the popular event will be announced later.

feed.title: Brown & Brown (BRO) Q3 2025 Earnings Transcript
feed.summary: Brown & Brown (BRO) reported strong Q3 2025 earnings with total revenues up 35.4% to $1.606 billion and adjusted EPS increasing over 15% to $1.05. The company's growth was significantly boosted by seven acquisitions, including Accession, contributing to a 3.5% organic revenue growth. Despite strong performance, the company anticipates slower organic growth in its Specialty Distribution segment for Q4 due to tough comparisons and headwinds in flood and lender-placed businesses, while the Retail segment expects growth similar to Q3.

feed.title: Principal Financial Group (NYSE:PFG) CEO Deanna Strable-Soethout Sells 8,025 Shares
feed.summary: Principal Financial Group (NYSE:PFG) CEO Deanna Strable-Soethout sold 8,025 shares of company stock for approximately $742,794, reducing her holding by nearly 13%. The stock is trading near its 52-week high, with a market capitalization of around $20.6 billion. Analyst sentiment is mixed, with an average "Reduce" rating and a target price of $92.88.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Halliburton (NYSE:HAL) CEO Sells $5,985,152.00 in Stock
feed.summary: Halliburton CEO Jeffrey Allen Miller sold 171,200 shares of the company's stock for $5,985,152.00 on January 23rd, decreasing his direct ownership by 13.45%. This sale occurred shortly after Halliburton reported strong quarterly earnings, beating expectations with an EPS of $0.69 and revenue of $5.66 billion. The company maintains a "Moderate Buy" rating from analysts and pays a quarterly dividend.

feed.title: Earnings Preview: Flushing Financial
feed.summary: Flushing Financial (NASDAQ: FFIC) is set to release its quarterly earnings report on Tuesday, January 27, 2026, with analysts forecasting an EPS of $0.35. Investors will be closely watching for results that surpass expectations and positive guidance for the upcoming quarter. The company has a history of beating EPS estimates, leading to positive share price movements, and its shares have seen a 6.91% increase over the last 52 weeks.

feed.title: Sarepta Therapeutics, Lands' End, USA Rare Earth, CoreWeave And Other Big Stocks Moving Higher On Monday
feed.summary: Several stocks experienced significant gains on Monday, with Sarepta Therapeutics Inc. jumping after releasing positive Phase 3 study results for its Duchenne muscular dystrophy treatment. Lands' End Inc. also saw a sharp increase following an announced joint venture, while various precious metals and rare-earth mineral stocks performed well amid market concerns and government interest. CoreWeave Inc. rose due to an expanded collaboration with Nvidia Corporation.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Blue Owl Capital Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against Blue Owl Capital Inc. (NYSE: OWL) on behalf of investors who purchased securities between February 6, 2025, and November 16, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that Blue Owl Capital made materially false and misleading statements and failed to disclose liquidity issues related to BDC redemptions. Investors who suffered losses are encouraged to join the case by February 2, 2026, to potentially be appointed as lead plaintiff.

feed.title: DEADLINE ALERT for DEFT, OWL, BTDR, and GAUZ: The Law
feed.summary: The Law Offices of Frank R. Cruz has issued a deadline alert for shareholders of DeFi Technologies Inc. (DEFT), Blue Owl Capital Inc. (OWL), Bitdeer Technologies Group (BTDR), and Gauzy Ltd. (GAUZ). Class action lawsuits have been filed against these companies, alleging misleading statements and failure to disclose material adverse facts. Investors suffering losses are encouraged to contact the firm to discuss their legal rights and participate in the class actions before the upcoming lead plaintiff deadlines.

feed.title: Marcus & Millichap Brokers Sale of Comfort Suites Sarasota Siesta Key
feed.summary: Marcus & Millichap has successfully brokered the sale of the 97-room Comfort Suites Sarasota Siesta Key in Sarasota, Florida. The transaction generated significant competitive interest due to the market's strength and the hotel's performance, despite recent regional challenges from hurricanes. This sale is seen as a positive indicator for Florida's hotel investment market.

feed.title: Chipotle Mexican Grill opens Schertz location
feed.summary: Chipotle Mexican Grill has opened its first location in Schertz, Texas, on January 26. This new restaurant offers customizability for burritos, bowls, and salads, and includes a drive-thru pick-up lane for digital orders. It also features the chain's recently launched protein-focused menu.

feed.title: Principal Financial Group CEO Strable-Soethout sells $742k in shares
feed.summary: Deanna D. Strable-Soethout, CEO of Principal Financial Group, sold 8,025 shares of common stock for approximately $742,794 on January 22, 2026, as part of a pre-planned trading arrangement. Despite this sale, she retains significant holdings, and the company recently surpassed Q3 2025 earnings expectations, though JPMorgan downgraded its stock rating to Neutral.

feed.title: TD Cowen Bullish on Constellation Energy (CEG) Amid Heightened Regulatory Risks and Recently Completed Calpine Acquisition
feed.summary: TD Cowen has initiated coverage on Constellation Energy (CEG) with a 'Buy' rating and a $440 price target, highlighting the recently completed $16.4 billion Calpine acquisition as a key growth catalyst. This acquisition, along with gas PPA agreements and the existing nuclear fleet, is expected to significantly boost annual free cash flow and marks progress in regulatory approvals with recent license renewals for clean energy centers. The firm acknowledges heightened regulatory risks but sees strong upside potential for the company.

feed.title: Mixed analyst sentiment on Vistra (VST) amid increasing regulatory risk and long-term contract wins
feed.summary: The article discusses the mixed analyst sentiment regarding Vistra (VST), a power generation and retail energy company. It highlights concerns about increasing regulatory risks, particularly in Texas, while also noting positive developments such as Vistra's success in securing long-term contracts. This creates a complex outlook for the company's future performance.

feed.title: Raymond James bullish on Cameco (CCJ) due to structural appeal of copper and uranium
feed.summary: Raymond James has reiterated its strong buy rating on Cameco (CCJ), increasing its price target from $60 to $63, driven by a positive outlook on the long-term structural demand for copper and uranium. The firm's analyst believes Cameco is a prime investment to capitalize on growth in both the clean energy transition and global electrification. This revised target reflects a 30% upside from its previous close, indicating strong confidence in the company's future performance.

feed.title: Wolfe Research bullish on Quanta Services (PWR) amid accelerating power demand
feed.summary: Wolfe Research has reiterated its Outperform rating on Quanta Services (PWR), citing accelerated power demand as a major tailwind. Analyst Nigel Coe believes that Quanta is a key beneficiary of the growing need for new power generation and expanded grid capacity, especially with increasing data center demand. The firm also highlights Quanta's strong backlog growth and attractive valuation compared to its peers.

feed.title: AT&T (T) Rating Maintained by Wells Fargo, Price Target Lowered to $27 | T Stock News
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has maintained its "Overweight" rating for AT&T (T) but lowered its price target from $29 to $27, following a trend of downward adjustments by other analysts. This indicates a cautious outlook on AT&T's future performance, despite its significant market share in the wireless and fixed-line services sectors. The average price target from 26 analysts is $28.66, suggesting a potential upside, while GuruFocus estimates a fair value of $20.95, implying a downside.

feed.title: Johnson & Johnson Stock: 2026 Outlook After 50% Gains
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) has shown resilient performance, posting $25 billion in quarterly revenue and $6 billion profit, driven by its pharmaceutical and MedTech portfolios. Despite challenges like tariffs and litigation, the stock is projected to reach $277 by 2028, implying a 26% total return or 8% annualized over three years. This outlook is based on assumptions of 5.8% revenue growth, 34.4% operating margins, and an 18.4x exit P/E multiple, with different scenarios explored for varying operational outcomes.

feed.title: American Express Inks $17.5 Million Post-Verdict Antitrust Deal
feed.summary: American Express has reached a $17.5 million settlement in an antitrust lawsuit regarding its anti-steering rules, five months after a mixed jury verdict. The proposed deal, if approved by the US District Court for the Eastern District of New York, would supersede a previous $12.5 million jury verdict and conclude a lawsuit initiated in 2019. The plaintiffs alleged that Amex's rules prevented merchants from directing customers to competing payment cards.

feed.title: Citizens Financial Group Announces Redemption of Subordinated Notes
feed.summary: Citizens Financial Group, Inc. announced the redemption of all its outstanding 4.300%, 3.750%, and 4.350% Fixed Rate Reset Subordinated Notes due February 11, 2031, with the redemption scheduled for February 11, 2026. The redemption price will be 100% of the principal amount plus accrued and unpaid interest. The company, one of the nation's largest financial institutions, will provide notice of the redemption and process details to noteholders.

feed.title: Diversified Energy TR-1
feed.summary: This TR-1 form notifies that BlackRock, Inc. has crossed a major holding threshold in Diversified Energy PLC. As of January 23, 2026, BlackRock's total voting rights in Diversified Energy PLC are 5.74%, a slight decrease from the previous notification's 5.75%. The document details the breakdown of voting rights attached to shares and through financial instruments, along with the full chain of controlled undertakings of BlackRock.

feed.title: Alexandria Real Estate Equities Investors Class Action Notice
feed.summary: The article announces a class action lawsuit against Alexandria Real Estate Equities for investors who purchased securities between January 27, 2025, and October 27, 2025. This legal action follows the company's disappointing Q3 FY2025 financial results, a significant cut in full-year FFO guidance, and a $323.9 million impairment charge, which led to a substantial stock price drop. The Portnoy Law Firm is offering free case evaluations to affected investors.

feed.title: Analysts Remain Mixed on EQT (EQT) Ahead of Fourth-Quarter Results
feed.summary: Analysts are presenting mixed sentiments toward EQT Corporation (NYSE:EQT) ahead of its fourth-quarter results, scheduled for February 17, 2026. Barclays reiterated an 'Overweight' rating but lowered its price target, citing resilience in the upstream sector despite commodity uncertainty. Bank of America Securities also reiterated a 'Buy' rating but reduced its price target due to rising oversupply risks in natural gas and revised price forecasts for 2027.

feed.title: 49-Cent Clearance Sale Arrives at Ross Stores This Week
feed.summary: Ross Dress for Less is holding its annual 49-cent clearance sale this week, a highly anticipated event by shoppers. The sale, which happens at the end of Ross's fiscal year in January, features items as low as 49 cents, including clothing, shoes, and home decor. Shoppers are advised to look for pinkish-purplish 49-cent tags and green price tags, as store employees confirm markdowns occur on Mondays, with the lowest prices appearing at the end of January.

feed.title: Jefferies Remains Cautious on NextEra Energy (NEE) As Eyes Turn to Execution
feed.summary: Jefferies has reiterated a 'Hold' rating on NextEra Energy (NEE) while slightly lowering its price target from $88 to $87, citing valuation discipline. The firm anticipates 8%+ EPS growth and sees large data center-related power contracts as a key catalyst for 2026. Jefferies is now focusing on execution, exemplified by NextEra Energy's recent finalization of 2.5 GW clean energy agreements with Meta Platforms.

feed.title: Procter & Gamble acquires Austin-based health product firm
feed.summary: Procter & Gamble (P&G) has completed its first acquisition of 2026, purchasing Austin-based consumer health product firm, Wonderbelly. The article, written by Sahar Chmais of the Austin Business Journal, highlights this strategic move by the Cincinnati-based giant. Wonderbelly was co-founded by brothers Noah Kraft and Lucas Kraft.

feed.title: NGL Energy Partners (NYSE:NGL) Reaches New 12-Month High - Should You Buy?
feed.summary: NGL Energy Partners (NYSE:NGL) reached a new 52-week high, trading as high as $10.38 and closing at $10.47. Analyst sentiment is mixed but leans positive, with Zacks upgrading the stock to a "strong-buy." Despite a negative P/E ratio, the company exceeded revenue estimates in its latest quarter, and institutional investors hold a significant portion of its shares.

feed.title: JPMorgan ETF List
feed.summary: This article provides a comprehensive list of JPMorgan ETFs traded in the USA, detailing their total assets, expense ratios, and various performance metrics. It also includes an overview of ETF Database features for researching and comparing ETFs, and outlines how JPMorgan ranks within ETF brand league tables based on revenue, fund flow, AUM, expense ratios, and dividend yields.

feed.title: Nordic American Tankers (NYSE:NAT) Sets New 52-Week High - Still a Buy?
feed.summary: Nordic American Tankers (NYSE: NAT) recently reached a new 52-week stock high of $4.23, although analysts maintain a consensus "Hold" rating with an average price target of $3.25. The company increased its quarterly dividend to $0.13, resulting in a high yield of 12.8%, but also reported an EPS miss and a remarkably high payout ratio of 2,600%, raising concerns about dividend sustainability. Institutional investors have notably increased their holdings in NAT.

feed.title: Kratos Expands Hypersonic Infrastructure as VisionWave Unveils Space-Based Counter-UAS Architecture
feed.summary: The defense sector is seeing a significant shift towards integrated RF and autonomous sensing systems, with VisionWave Holdings detailing its Argus space-enabled counter-UAS architecture. Simultaneously, Kratos Defense & Security Solutions has opened a new manufacturing hub for hypersonic systems to support the MACH-TB 2.0 program. This convergence of advanced hypersonic capabilities and space-based AI-driven detection aims to enhance national security by providing wide-area threat awareness and rapid neutralization.

feed.title: Vivian announces partnership with Novartis Pharma Services AG in Malta
feed.summary: Vivian has formed a distribution agreement with Novartis Pharma Services AG, a global R&D pharmaceutical company. This partnership solidifies Vivian's role in importing and distributing Novartis products in Malta, bridging international pharmaceutical innovation with the local healthcare system. The collaboration highlights Vivian's strong regulatory framework, governance, and infrastructure, ensuring world-class therapies reach Maltese patients with high operational and ethical standards.

feed.title: French Connection Group and G-III Apparel Group Announce Licensing Agreement for the North American Market
feed.summary: French Connection Group (FCG) has announced a long-term licensing agreement with G-III Apparel Group to develop and distribute men's and women's apparel and accessories in North America, effective February 1, 2026. This partnership aims to accelerate French Connection's growth in the North American market, leveraging G-III's expertise and retail relationships to expand the brand's global presence. The agreement is a strategic step in FCG's revitalization and global expansion strategy following its takeover by MIP Holdings in 2021.

feed.title: Tempered Earnings Outlook And Deutsche Bank Downgrade Might Change The Case For Investing In Sherwin-Williams (SHW)
feed.summary: Sherwin-Williams (SHW) is facing increased investor caution after a Deutsche Bank downgrade to Hold, citing valuation concerns and dampened earnings growth prospects. Despite some analyst support for restructuring and pricing initiatives, the softened consensus expectations for Q4 2025 results and doubts about an earnings beat challenge the premium valuation previously held by the company. The article suggests investors should carefully review multiple perspectives before making investment decisions, especially given the balanced growth expectations and valuation.

feed.title: Raymond James Bullish on Cameco (CCJ) Due to Structural Appeal of Copper and Uranium
feed.summary: Raymond James has raised its price target for Cameco Corporation (CCJ) to C$165, reiterating an 'Outperform' rating due to the structural appeal of copper and uranium amid tightening supply. Bernstein also increased its price target for Cameco, citing uranium's tightening supply, rising term prices, and policy-driven nuclear expansion, viewing nuclear as a stable AI proxy for powering data centers. Both firms anticipate structurally higher uranium prices and favor Cameco's position as a large-scale, low-cost producer with broad fuel-cycle exposure.

feed.title: 3 Durable Stocks to Buy Now for the Long Term
feed.summary: This article discusses key stock market topics as presented in "The Morning Filter" podcast, celebrating its third anniversary. Hosts Dave Sekera and Susan Dziubinski revisit past stock picks, discuss current market volatility, and welcome Sarah Hansen to preview the upcoming Federal Reserve meeting. The episode also covers upcoming earnings reports from major companies like Microsoft, Meta, Apple, and Tesla, and provides insights on Netflix and Berkshire Hathaway's potential moves.

feed.title: More than 85% of Dominion Energy customers have power restored after winter storm
feed.summary: Dominion Energy has restored power to over 85% of customers affected by a recent winter storm. Despite lower-than-forecasted ice accumulation, strong winds and remaining ice on trees could still cause additional outages. Crews are facing challenges due to icy roads and fallen trees, but the system is prepared for increased demand amidst upcoming frigid temperatures.

feed.title: Scotiabank Constructive on The Williams Companies (WMB) Amid Broader Reset of Energy Infrastructure Valuations
feed.summary: Scotiabank has raised its price target for The Williams Companies (WMB) to $66 from $61, maintaining a 'Sector Perform' rating, citing a broader reset of energy infrastructure valuations driven by increasing power demand and LNG exports. UBS also reiterated a 'Buy' rating with a $78 price target due to accelerating progress on the Northeast Supply Enhancement and Constitution pipeline projects, which are expected to significantly boost the company's EBITDA. The Williams Companies focuses on natural gas infrastructure, with these projects slated for completion by Q4 2027 and April 2028, respectively.

feed.title: Eli Lilly or Viking Therapeutics: Which Stock Is More Likely to be a Millionaire-Maker?
feed.summary: This article compares Eli Lilly and Viking Therapeutics, both major players in the rapidly expanding weight loss drug market. While Eli Lilly boasts blockbuster drugs and a high market cap, Viking Therapeutics offers significant growth potential due to its lower valuation and promising drug candidates in clinical trials. The piece explores which company has a higher likelihood of being a "millionaire-maker" stock, acknowledging the higher risk associated with Viking Therapeutics due to its clinical development stage.
